\noindent The achieved assurance is calculated using the
non-central \(t\) distribution. A material
overstatement shifts the distribution of the
standardized difference to the right. A material
understatement shifts it to the left.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
# Achieved assurance when controlling efficiency risk
assurance_over = 1 - nct.cdf(c_U, df=df, nc=delta)
assurance_under = nct.cdf(c_L, df=df, nc=-delta)
print("Achieved assurance for detecting overstatement:",
    np.round(assurance_over * 100, 1), "%\n")
print("Achieved assurance for detecting understatement:",
    np.round(assurance_under * 100, 1), "%\n")
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
Achieved assurance for detecting overstatement: 36.9 %

Achieved assurance for detecting understatement: 36.9 %
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
